title: Tight Entropic Uncertainty Relations
abstract: Entropic uncertainty relations $H(A)+H(B)\geqslant γ$ give a nonzero lower bound $γ$ to the sum of the Shannon entropies $H$ of the outcome probabilities of incompatible observables $A$ and $B$. They are better than the variance-based uncertainty relations because they only depend on the Born statistics of the outcomes and not on the outcomes themselves, and because bounds $γ$ typically are state independent. Here we provide a state-independent lower bound $γ_s$ that is better than the textbook Maassen-Uffink bound and, in the limit of the parameter $s\to 2$, becomes asymptotically tight for all $A,B$. The bound can be extended to Renyi entropies.
summary: This paper introduces a new, tighter state-independent lower bound for entropic uncertainty relations that outperforms the standard Maassen-Uffink bound. By using the full overlap matrix instead of just the maximum overlap, the new bound becomes asymptotically tight in a specific limit. The result is applicable to both Shannon and Renyi entropies.
main_problem: Improving the lower bound of entropic uncertainty relations (EUR) to be tighter than the standard Maassen-Uffink bound by utilizing the full structure of the overlap matrix.
main_result: The authors derive a new state-independent lower bound that is strictly stronger than the Maassen-Uffink bound and becomes asymptotically tight as a parameter s approaches 2. This bound can also be extended to Renyi entropies.
method: The derivation utilizes the Riesz-Thorin Interpolation Theorem and the Nonlinear Power Iteration Method (NPIM) for calculating operator norms, with Monte Carlo simulations used for validation.
model_or_system: The study considers general quantum systems described by two incompatible observables, A and B, where the relationship between their eigenbases is defined by a unitary overlap matrix U.
key_observables: Shannon entropy, Renyi entropy, and the overlap matrix elements between bases.
important_parameters: The parameter s in the interval (1, 2), the conjugate parameter s', and the rotation angle phi for the qubit case.
main_assumptions: The bound is state-independent, meaning it applies to all possible quantum states of the system.
figures_summary: Figure 1 compares the new bound against Maassen-Uffink in large dimensions; Figure 2 compares NPIM and Monte Carlo methods for calculating norms; Figure 3 shows the qubit-specific bound as a function of the rotation angle phi, highlighting a transition at a critical angle.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of EUR bounds, presents the new derivation using Riesz-Thorin interpolation, extends the result to Renyi entropies, provides a specific analysis for qubits, and validates the results through numerical methods and Monte Carlo simulations.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for quantum information because tighter uncertainty relations are fundamental to quantifying information processing limits, security in quantum key distribution, and characterizing the precision of quantum measurements.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
